channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: How is your career going? — 89 replies — 356.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How is your career going?

Open thread • Replies: 89 • Updated: 2026-06-23 08:04 UTC

OP

I make $28/hr as a Fire Alarm Inspection Technician. I have 2 licenses and 4.5 years of experience:
-FASA/BASA
-NICET Inspection and Testing Level 2

Early replies (first 3)

62381055
Going for the NICET Fire Alarm Systems Level 1 license in the next 2 months or so. Will hopefully get to $30/hr.

62381060
>>62381049
*chirp*

62381078
>>62381049
is that a fun job? sounds comfy desu

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62384198
>>62384184
Depends on CoL in your area, but it's better than most.

62384480
$50k cad customer support
36 yrs old
I am severely underperforming but I love making music -- married a woman from a well off family, we have a house
Confidence low, bros, confidence low

62384785
>>62384480
As it should be, you're the woman in the relationship.

62385873
>>62384785
Not exactly, but maybe to an extent as I am an artistic man. I'm definitely the defender though.

Not sure how to upgrade but also keep up with my artistry which I feel is my greatest passion and inspiration

At the very least I feel teaching your children music and related science opens up their mind to a lot of thinking

62385905
I make sandwitch

32 per hour aud 48 hr weeks

62388145
>>62381049
GSE Fleet mechanic for an airline 38$ an hour 60$ an hour for service calls

62388200
>>62381049
I'm 39m and a farmer. My dad retired about 10 years ago and I have taken over everything since then. Before then I had been working for him since I could walk pretty much. I remember the time before roundup ready beans and I spent most of my summers pulling weeds.

I made about $250k last year and my living expenses are about $10k. I live in town and own one of the cheapest houses in the county because my dad wouldn't let me live in a yurt in the grove. I drive a '78 F-150 I paid $400 for when I was in high school.

Anyways outside of planting and harvest I work about 3 hours per day and spend the rest of the day shitposting, checking my stocks, playing playstation 2, watching my sitcom DVD collection, and playing a round of golf at the local muni.

62388215
>>62381049
I make 60$ an hour to respond to passive aggressive emails
I got one waiting in my inbox rn.

62388275
$16 an hour at a dead end non-tech job even though I have a degree and some certs. I am the go to IT guy in my family which sometimes pays, the other day I installed a new hard drive and cleaned the pc for a family member. I didn't even ask for money since it was such a simple task but she gave me 60 bucks for it since that is what a store was going to charge her.

62388285
>>62381049
110k senior security consultant, wfh, unlimited pto, 4 years at the global company, burned out for the last 1.5 years but can't afford to quit because I got the job straight out of college through internship and I have no idea how to get another job because I refuse to do any certs or practice interviewing.

62388291
>>62388275
Why do you not have a job with your degree? Are you applying?

62388337
>>62388200
I envy the cheap pickups people can buy in farm country. on Long Island everything succumbs to rust almost immediately or is sold within the closed Spanish-speaking illegal community, so is impossible to buy.

62388396
>>62388291
Yea I've sent out over 350 applications. I've gotten my resume reviewed and edited by the alumni career services team, family members, and people in the industry. I have a home lab and document everything. I am not being picky and am willing to even take a job that pays less if it's in something tech related. Honestly it's gotten to the point where I'm close to giving up and just going to door knock for Andrew Yang so I get UBI.

62388428
39$/hour as a masonry laborer.

I started at 25$/hr in the company’s office, where I used my tech skills to build website stuff, learned and ran Google Ads and got some work for the company, which progressively rewarded me with raises.

I ran out of work at some point due to it being a small company so I was put in the field as a laborer. Now I do grunt work but get paid the same.

Although I’m not “growing”, this job feels like most feasible path for me to build my own construction company. I’ve already done jobs on the side and have my LLC open, website, SEO going on.

I want to scale my company to 10-15k/month net, but have been running into lots of cheap people when estimating jobs. It is a game of consistency, and sooner rather than later I can see my GBP with many reviews bringing me an organic pipeline of jobs, as well as eventual institutional/commercial contacts I meet here and there giving me consistent and higher margin work.

62388494
>>62381049
It ain't

62388513
>Consultant
I don't even care about the salary or the career as long as I can be there to axe working class workers.

62389047
>>62381049
I make 21$ an hour on welfare. 35k$ a year as a permaneet
I al thinking of buying an Ioniq 5 N-Line in gold
The reason I am ok the dole is because I have a booboo in my mental state snd alo a tiny, unused weenis

62391208
>>62382899
>>62382522
>>62383085
I did something similar 10 years ago while still in high school, helping small business troubleshoot IT. One of them hired me full-time at 48k/yr. I quit after a year and a half bc 48k/yr. I should not have taken the job, low ambition / lazy decision on my part. I then proceeded to waste 10 years of my life with a drinking problem. I’m good now, working construction. Only physical labor could cure my fake & gay depression / alcoholism. I want to get back into IT but have not touched a computer since. What kind of IT problems are you solving on what kind of computer systems / networks? Could you give me a list or some examples of the types of problems you are solving? And any recommendations on getting back up to speed, skills, training, etc? I’m good with sales, finding business opportunities, but my IT skills have massively atrophied. Any help would be appreciated. Thanks in advance

62391409
Bump

Bisraelis
United
Must
Prevail

62391963
Deep protruding anal bump

62392151
Its going bad. Unemployed for a couple years now.

62392298
>tfw they are starting to catch onto my mistakes/half assing at work that I've been hiding and sweeping under the rug

62393651
22.50/hour as a retail wagie. Hours are super unreliable, but I've been getting 40/week pretty consistently and I get full benefits. I live with my parents so money wise I'm doing great, but in my soul I'm suffering. I hate this job, I hate working, I hate having to structure my life around it, I hate the people I meet, I hate the people I work with. Hate hate hate hate.

I've been trying to move up the food chain for a year now with no luck. At least my parents are sympathetic

62394051
>>62381719
I make $43 an hour as a system administrator

62397846
Anon from the last week's small business thread here: I recently finished my basic ESL teaching toolkit, eta for a complementary worksheet student book and teacher's manual around mid-August.

62399001
$42 an hour controls engineer with double roll as plc programming. Most of the time my life is a puzzle adventure and then a few times a year my service trips dealing with integrating with some off the wall communication protocol squeezes my butthole when in not familiar with it. I enjoy it but i wish i could go to school to learn more on the programming side. Started as an electrical panel technician 15 years ago, and worked my way up.

62399771
>Get rejection email
>The templated mail says it's not the news you were hoping for and that they won't give individual feedback due to volume
Rude >:(

62399802
>>62381049
I work as a hospital janitor making $34 dollars an hour.

62399819
I make 164k at one job, 150k at another, and 33k at my third, plus random side jobs here and there. So...347k net income. Gross is 5200 a paycheck from one, 990 from another, and 4000 from another, so I'm clearing around....20k a month? Plus a 13k yearly bonus which is....253k after taxes.

62399834
>>62381049
I'm a data engineer.
I make $150k, plus bonus.
Most of you are probably too retarded to do what I do.

item.title: What made you still hold BTC? — 101 replies — 77.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What made you still hold BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 101 • Updated: 2026-06-23 06:46 UTC

OP

Bitcoin has gone through multiple cycles, crashes, and endless criticism, yet many continue to hold. What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

Early replies (first 3)

62371733
>>62371718
>What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

62371807
The only reason to ever sell bitcoin is to buy more at a lower price.

62371917
>>62371807
correct
>waiting for a good entry

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62387534
>>62387122
We'll know for sure once TBV launches on mainnet. That's when we'll see if it gains adoption and makes a real impact.

62387549
>>62383732
If you buy memecoins, not $btc

62387563
>>62387534
Defi is no longer valuable

62387566
Im fine

62387833
>>62387563
Depends what you mean by valuable. A lot of the hype is gone, but people are still borrowing, lending, and farming yield.

62387836
>>62387549
Doge coins lol

62387875
>>62371718
Because it's the only crypto asset I'd be comfortable holding for the next decade without constantly checking the chart.

62387892
the longer this market exists, the more Bitcoin seems to become the benchmark everything else gets compared against. So very very bullish

62388642
>>62387875
100%

62388704
>>62371718
Institutions are starting to make it into BTC, before bridge was the issue but with TBV eliminating that, more money will start flowing into BTC, it still feels early.

62388780
>>62376610
> printing is the only thing keeping the bubble from popping instantly. you want that?

62388819
>>62377444
> nothing has changed except the price chart and your cope levels

62388854
nothing, im not retarded so i take profits. my cost basis is negative now.

62389149
>>62388780
You know my answer for that

62389340
I feel like something new has to happen before the market shifts sentiment.

All the ETFs and government regulation just hurt the project.
Nobody is utilizing the tools, the tools still feel beta, and developers are being arrested.

If it's just going to be a big gay digital rock the momentum grinds to a halt.

The problem is some of us got rich and have something to lose now.
Different factions formed inside the developer community.
Is there incentive to free ball the network still? idk.

I'm still holding but this bear feels different

62390164
>>62388819
So how will you change it?

62391118
>>62389340
I'm still holding as well.

62391156
>>62371718
It feels like shit when it goes down but when you're near the bottom like now you just remember the last times you felt this way and how it gongo up from here, everytime. Bobos are ngmi. Mumu supremacy is real. Cope trannies.

62391165
>>62383619
Based retard

62391192
>>62371718
The 20% long-term-capital-gains tax rate that I'd have to pay if I cashed out all at once.

62391471
The only investments I hold are schizo nonsense Chainlink and PMs. I have a good amount of money through my Roth and 401k. I can only invest in specific etfs through my work 401k. What’s the best method to get long term Bitcoin exposure through a IRA?

62392524
>>62391156
You're not a real investor

62393205
>>62372380
Then how come I can send BTC to my Bitcoin core wallet dumbfuck?

62393451
>>62371718
I believe in the philosophy that led to bitcoin. Sadly the vast majority of humanity are just cattle. I still hold because I believe there is something that can happen. The pessimism on this board doesn't change my view on this. I will hold and if it fails I'll be ok because I have stocks and silver and land.

62395366
>>62391192
That's too much to be honest

62395566
>>62371718
I plan on selling when I retire in ten years. But who knows maybe I will change my mind and keep holding.

62396187
All these retards are greedy, that's all there is to it

62398052
>>62395566
Keep going anon

62399672
>>62396187
Oh really huh?

62399688
>>62371718
I forgot the passwords for one account and everything got stolen from the other.

item.title: I'm trapped in a tutorial level in life. — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I'm trapped in a tutorial level in life.

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-23 07:54 UTC

OP

I'm trapped in a tutorial level in life.

>zero work but deadend WFH job
>no social life at all, too ugly to get a date and too autist to fit in anywhere
>in my 30s and missed out on all 20s socialising
>live in Britain and watch and listen to stories of people getting rich in America
>go on holidays and just walk about, never talk to anyone, visiting museums
>financially, saving money makes most sense, but haven't saved enough to not have to work, don't earn enough to spend money on anything interesting
>no technical skills, no expertise, doing anything would take huge amounts of effort and likely lead to no outcome

I am a spectator in this world.

Early replies (first 3)

62398493
>>62398482
The older I get the more I don't understand how more people haven't gotten violent about the state of things. It's extremely bleak out there.

62398521
>>62398482
>doing anything would take huge amounts of effort and likely lead to no outcome
well yeah, that's life. start by expending some effort to figure out what you want to do, what interests you. then continue expending effort doing that thing

62398532
I’ve had a very similar life but I’m American and 42. I did work in an office but have been remote since the lockdowns started. only difference really is I got to save and invest.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62398608
>>62398493
Life is easier than ever. You are so rich you have time to cry on an incel blog about imaginary problems

62398688
>>62398482
>>62398493
>>62398532
White harder.

62398732
>>62398608
Meanwhile in reality where everyone is rich and everything is easier than ever:
https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=6lFXmDk-tps

62398861
>>62398732
It is easier to get rich, but losers like you dont even try you just look for excuses and cry on incel blogs while posting government media to soothe yourself

62398903
>>62398608
>Life is easier than ever. You are so rich you have time to cry on an incel blog
sure
>about imaginary problems
ok you got me, i thought your post was serious for a sec.

62398924
>>62398903
OK chicken little, here's an example>>62398732 this idiot is worried about birth rates when he himself is a poor incel crying his life away. Go outside and take what you want all your competition is scared losers crying in their basement

62398956
>>62398493
I already have lol, and will escalate it more and more

62399052
>>62398956
Enjoy prison, tard.

62399065
>>62398493
OP barely has to work and has housing and is able to take vacations and you think that is bleak?

I was reading about what life was like in China in the 70s and it was common for the children to have lice and get frostbite in the winters, you whiny babies are so priveleged and yet you just want more more more.

62399814
>>62399065
>our problems are diminutive because third world bug people got frostbitten
This is the same as crying about niggers in africa starving, who fucking cares? The West is so advanced because White people made it so, that doesn't mean problems Westerns face in the West aren't actual problems.

item.title: How am I ever supposed to get married and start a family if 95% of people are horrible with money? — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How am I ever supposed to get married and start a family if 95% of people are horrible with money?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-23 08:02 UTC

OP

How am I ever supposed to get married and start a family if 95% of people are horrible with money? The last girl I dated was nice but 4 months into the relationship mentioned having thousands in credit card debt and student loans for a degree she didn't even finish. The girl I dated before that never had any savings and was always impulse buying stupid shit. I worked hard to stay debt free and save money. I don't want to pick up the tab for someone just for them to divorce me anyway. Don't give me some BS about its my duty to be a "male provider." I just feel like I'm never going to find someone who ticks my other boxes and isn't a financial retard landmine.

Early replies (first 3)

62399691
>>62399687
>I just feel like I'm never going to find someone who ticks my other boxes and isn't a financial retard landmine.
Then do not date?

62399745
>>62399687
dont get married. its a meme, and even if they aren't financially insolvent, they can simply divorce your ass whenever they choose and confiscate your wealth.
consider any other contract. imagine signing a contract where if the OTHER PARTY breaks the contract, they are entitled to rewards paid by you. does that sound fair? do you think that would be a good idea? because that is modern marriage.
https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=5e2vFc1AIOA

62399750
>>62399687
just dont care about money, then there's no problem

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62399764
No idea. I'm considering just becoming a passportbro.

62399766
>>62399687
It’s really hard to find someone good enough to have kids with, that’s for sure. My advice is always to essentially network your way into a decent lady. Make friends with decent people and successful couples, and then ask if they know anyone

62399813
>>62399687
Precisely why it should be easier.
>except for homosexuals
>nobody is proud of you

62399831
>>62399687
You marry a bitch richer than you, retard.

item.title: CZ is richer than Bill Gates — 17 replies — 16.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
CZ is richer than Bill Gates

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-23 07:02 UTC

OP

And the 19th richest person in the world. You're seriously trying to tell me crypto is dead?

Early replies (first 3)

62398500
>>62398496
yes? that's pretty much all stablecoins

62398527
>>62398500
No faggot, it's because he owns 90% of a $100B crypto exchange. Plus whatever else

62398541
>>62398496
It's dead because everyone but the exchanges are gone. They just spoof volume and trade between themselves whenever they come up with any new bullshit narratives. MUH privacy, MUH dexes, MUH quantum, etc.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62398554
>>62398527
yea, and of all exchanges, binance has the biggest stablecoin reserves ($50 billion). at least half his wealth is not crypto

62398577
>>62398541
100% this, and it’s what I’ve been saying for a long time. Just look at all the top tokens and they all have 9+ figures in daily volume. The whole market is manipulated. It’s all fake and gay. Anyone who argues otherwise is a desperate bag holder screaming into the void. No one is coming to save them

62398601
>>62398554
CIRCL technically has 75 billion in stablecoin reserves but only has a 20 billion dollar valuation. Keep saying dumb shit, it makes me feel smart

62398606
>>62398601
circle doesn't own all of their aum silly. binance does own their stables. but you bring up another point that valuing binance with a hard number is a retarded exercise

62398652
>>62398500
> so you're telling me a digital dollar is just a dollar with extra steps and no fdic

62398705
>>62398496
Nigger sold everyone’s bitcoin and killed the altcoin season. He deserves nothing. The only justice would be him going bankrupt buying back all the bitcoins he sold that wasn’t his.
Scamnance wasn’t even subtle about it either. Fuckers sold all of the crypto and had them all go to 0 literally. For weeks people couldn’t withdraw from scamnance because they didn’t have the coins. Scamnance should be wiped off the face of the earth. Piece of shit scam exchange.

62398935
>>62398496
Scammer. Dont cex!

62398946
>>62398541
Everyone went onchain, check ethereums stats!

62398950
>>62398946
Hence the atl cex balance

62398957
>>62398496
Cexes are so 2018! Fuck cz and all the leeches!

62399007
I dont believe this for one second

62399038
>>62399007
That he sold "your" coins for stabkes? Do a binance wintermute googling then!

62399095
>>62398496
He got rich shorting crypto kek

62399719
>>62399095
Liquidating fags with user fund movements to wm and back plus scamming with shitcoin orwcles like on 10/10 and many more jewish tricks.

item.title: The quality of the English on this board has gone to shit. — 19 replies — 8.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
The quality of the English on this board has gone to shit.

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-06-23 05:46 UTC

OP

The quality of the English on this board has gone to shit. Is it the same ESL jeet or are we being invaded?

Early replies (first 3)

62393333
>>62393333

62393335
>>62393333
How did he do it...

62393345
>>62393332
A horse that good doesnt jumping haver that it have to be

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62393357
>>62393332
Indians are financial experts you would have wisdom to be listening and learning.

62393365
>>62393333
What a nigger Sunday this day has turned out to be.

62393369
>>62393333
No fucking way...

62393370
>>62393333
hey i keep clicking it but can't see the reply

62393375
>>62393333
what does this mean?

62393376
>>62393332
Also, what is that frog doing?

62393416
>>62393376
Watching. Me fapping.

62393447
>>62393332
>Is it the same ESL jeet
Sorry bro, it's mostly just me. But I never try to hide the fact that I'm a thirdie. I wouldn't mind if /biz/ had flags.

62393462
>>62393333
¿Qué es esta brujería?

62393473
>>62393333
Tell me more

62395023
>>62393332
It's either ESL or incel.

62395265
>>62393357
Indians are diarrhea drinking experts.

62395357
>>62393332
Hello saar. Please delete this thread saar. Praise Vishnu saar.

62395358
>>62393375
It means you're gay.

62399555
>>62393333
lol.
OP is Neo.

62399567
>>62393333
I'd like to see a jeet pull this off

item.title: When can you start bragging about your NW on /biz/ — 8 replies — 6.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
When can you start bragging about your NW on /biz/

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-23 06:55 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62399130
>>62399125
six figs

62399135
>>62399125
2014

62399141
if u werent in the top whatever % in some past year u will always be lower class or whatever the line is

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62399161
>>62399141
not if you trade NFTs well

62399176
7 figs

62399180
>>62399130

62399217
>>62399180

62399702
>>62399125
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 9 replies — 6.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-23 06:41 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62396002
Invest only in actual products made by actual companies.

62396879
>>62395695
People have been calling Bitcoin worthless for over a decade. At some point it's worth asking why demand keeps returning every cycle.

62398057
>>62395915
Because I can hold it for the long term while exploring new ways to put it to work.

62398163
>>62398057
Might as well do a bank transfer as it's more private, costs less and the money won't disappear into thin air.

62398819
>>62395655
Because it's not actual Bitcoin, unlike Bitcoin SV (ticker: BSV), which I'm holding.

62399676
>>62398819
BSV is super dead super super dead

item.title: GLOWIE ALERT GLOWIE ALERT GLOWIE ALERT GET THE FUCK OUT OF MSTR NOW!!! — 5 replies — 6.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
GLOWIE ALERT GLOWIE ALERT GLOWIE ALERT GET THE FUCK OUT OF MSTR NOW!!!

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-23 07:15 UTC

OP

GLOWIE ALERT GLOWIE ALERT GLOWIE ALERT

GET THE FUCK OUT OF MSTR NOW!!!
GLOWIES HAVE INFILTRATED MSTR

https://x.com/yourfriendSOMMI/status/2069247925084176500?s=20

Early replies (first 3)

62399648
Kino post. Based OP for once

62399659
It's all so tiring

62399669
>>62399631
Yeah its common knowledge. Lee is probably too.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62399740
>>62399631
lol take your meds schizo

62399744
>>62399740
Open your eyes kid, or go back to the reddit hive mind.

item.title: Why should I max out a Roth IRA and not just put 20% in that and 80% into buying calls 6+ months out and/or selling puts… — 16 replies — 4.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why should I max out a Roth IRA and not just put 20% in that and 80% into buying calls 6+ months out and/or selling puts…

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-23 04:42 UTC

OP

Why should I max out a Roth IRA and not just put 20% in that and 80% into buying calls 6+ months out and/or selling puts?

Early replies (first 3)

62394825
You should buy 0DTE OTM options

62394847
>>62394796
This is AI. Even people who do free climbing like this don't (can't) climb on smooth surfaces for that length.

62394859
>>62394847
Its not ai. Its a French freeclimber Alexis landing.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62394874
Because you don't know what you're doing and your expected rate of return into options plays will be lower than whatever safe shit is in your IRA.

62394882
>>62394874
I made 33% last year only selling puts otm. Much better than my 457b.

62394884
>>62394882
Oh cool. I guess that one year metric means you're good to go and will on average make more money than your IRA. Go for it!

62394897
>>62394884
I guess you're right. Its hard to see when you've only won. I only do it on profitable and safer companies like WMT and NVO etc. As well but any company can have massive downsides. I just want to retire before I'm well past my prime • •

62394934
>>62394796
Looks like a big garloid mating with a small garloid

62394944
>>62394897
All I'm saying is don't get too carried away with gambling based on recent historical results. It's probably fine to be a bit more aggressive than the average normie but a good amount of shmucks with the mindset you have think they are invincible and end up losing half their net worth in 5 years. Just be careful anon.

62394995
>>62394847
looks like there's a few videos of this guy as >>62394859 says. might be legit

62395174
>>62394796
Options are a zero sum game. For you to win, someone else has to lose, and you are up against people who are the best in the world at it and have vast computing and research assets at their disposal. It is hard to beat the forever tax-free gains of the Roth IRA/401k.

62395208
>>62394847
>>62394859
I love it when arrogant faggots get immediately buttblasted

62395712
You can just put the calls in your roth and have no tax on gains

62395776
>>62394796
Aww hell nawww.

62395792
>>62394796
man these videos always make my balls get sweaty

62399430
>>62394796
uuh

item.title: NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!! THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!! SOMEONE MUS… — 132 replies — 58.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!! THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!! SOMEONE MUS…

Open thread • Replies: 132 • Updated: 2026-06-23 05:49 UTC

OP

NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!!

THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!!

SOMEONE MUST PUT A STOP TO THIS NOW!!!!!!!!!!!! REDDIT ARMY RISE UP!!!!!!!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62375140
this is entirely sustainable, you have EDS if you disagree

62375150
>>62375140
Short it then, stupid nigger

62375163
>company called "Space Exploration Technologies"
>run by the first trillionaire

>you STILL didn't buy any of their stock

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62398094
>$154

62398098
>>62375128

62398122
>>62375128

62398136
>dumping like a shitcoin
MUSKMAN, TWEET ABOUT THE DOG COIN
DO SOMETHING!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

62398141
THE DUMP SHALL CONTINUE UNTIL MORALE IMPROVES

62398144
So who do we call to stop this? Can anyone stop the devaluation? This isn't funny

62398150
>>62375535
Trvke

62398178
YOU SAID I WAS GOING TO BE RICH
YOU SAID THIS WAS MY TICKET OUT OF THE PERMANENT UNDERCLASS

62398184
>>62398144
>This isn't funny

It's a hoot and a holler as far as i'm concerned

62398185
>>62398178
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're part of the permanent underclass

62398188
HAHAHAHAHA

62398204
>>62381795
He spends much more than 1 million a day, per his company income statement

62398211
>>62398185
>Collapse the dollar
>Now everyone is a millionaire :)

62398213
>>62381795
wtf is mann._michael13 on about?

62398226
bros im worried OP will tie the noose after getting pumped and dumped, we need to be much more reassuring to our friends that mistakes happen and we can overcome mistakes if we work together. Don't let op be another hanged man in the bathtub meme.

62398877
>>62392029
I hate the French

62398970
>>62398090
This animu was a fun watch. Hope you enjoyed it too schadenfreude bro

62398978
>>62375128
>2pbtid

62399080
>>62375150
Bet.

Guess my gains.

62399090
>>62375128
You were saying?

62399160
>>62375140
Well, think of it like this. The right half is all the economic activity that having a jetliner enabled for humanity. Space is vaster still than the air. Space can do what air can do, and more, we can get resources in space. So you have to imagine that the total economic potential of space can be as great if not greater than the economic activity from air travel.
Spacex is the closest of all companies to making it so that the biggest cost to sending something to space is just the fuel to do it. Can they achieve that? If so, then they have a the jetliner of space travel. It would take tens if not hundreds of billions, and at least a decade if not 2 or 3 to replicate what spacex has done, from scratch. Most of their competitors aren't starting from scratch, but they are way behind. If you wanted it done quickly, it would be very expensive, and perhaps not even possible to buy the capability that spacex presents itself capable of delivering.

62399168
>>62375128
The one cow village of OP.

62399196
>>62375128
It is absolutely uncanny how /biz/ calls the top like this.
Anons like OP are like a inverse oracles.

62399203
>>62399196
not really it was kinda obvious
its priced like a top 10 company but makes no money. Even it does start to make top 10 company profit the price wont go anywhere so yeah

62399214
Rip to all the jeets falling for elon scam. I guess your villages are going to starve in the next few months

62399292
>>62399203
Yeah you could expect spacex to go down, but the timing is always tied to gloat posting.
>OH NO NO NO
>YOU’RE SO DUMB FOR BEING BEARISH
>HA HA HA HA
You could almost set puts to this.

62399317
SPCX will drop to at least the IPO price in the near term. I wouldn't even consider buying it until that happens.

62399364
>>62375784
>You could have 10x or 100x real estate
>but you don't want to make it up into outer space

62399387
>>62375128

>>62398185

62399575
>>62399160

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 136 replies — 35.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 136 • Updated: 2026-06-23 04:16 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62393782
Also, as we near the end of the thread, there will be a debriefing and recap.

62393786
Unfortunately I misquoted, this anon
>>62378439

Is who said it best in regards to this post
>>62393776

62393801
Please keep all stock suggestions relevant to the discussion at hand

62393834
>>62392256
the same but at least im locked in. If I lose my job tomorrow and never invest another penny I'll still be fine to retire well before 65. that's peace of mind. 10 more years and my rentals will all be paid off and that will more than cover my existence costs.

>>62393449
>just make money
>just buy assets
yeah that's how 99.9% of everyone with actual money got it. biz loves to talk about how they made x billion on cumcoin yet in the real world thats akin to winning the lotto

62394075
LASR
IPGP
PLAB
QUBT
RGTI
IONQ
Nbis
IREN
AAOI
POET
SIVEF
SOITEC
SMHN
CRDO
ALMU
LASE
OPTX
SKYT


Some stock picks to look into in the world of photonics

62394345
>>62394075
>NBIS ($286.69)

-Too expensive for us

>CRDO ($271.83)

-Too expensive

>AAOI ($161.85)

-Too expensive

>SOITEC (Euro stock, $133.39)

-Too expensive

>IPGP ($118.01)

-IPG Photonics primary supplier of high-power fiber lasers and automated laser systems heavily utilized in Elon Musks TESLAs

>LASR ($64.73)

- **Laser Photonics (LASE)** – Develops industrial laser systems and is expanding into **anti-drone technology**."
>IREN ($59.96)

-Not relevant, disregard


>IONQ ($56.55)

-Moreso quantum related, not really photonics

>SKYT ($36.57)

-Recently bought by IONQ,


>RGTI ($21.36)

>>Reddit says:
-the most speculative, revenue is actually declining and the P/S is hard to defend at any timeline, the honest framing for all of them is these are binary outcome bets not compounders. fault tolerant quantum is 5-15 years out, they'll keep burning cash and diluting until then.

>POET ($12.15)

Pump ended

>QUBT ($10.76)

-They make the tlfn photonic chip. Where silicon based chips use electrons, their tfln chips use
photons to send information. First tfln chip foundry in the U.S.


>SIVEF ($10.12)

-Fidelity only, pushing to get US LISTING


>LASE ($2.32)

-We missed this pump

>OPTX ($12.11)

- We missed this pump

LYTE - Releasing August, from the same company that brought you DRAM.

FOTO - this is the ETF for photonics tickers, very over diversified however

62394373
>>62373752
Is there a market for books with alternative physics?

62394789
The third topic shall be future tech and science new

62394870
>>62392312

Go learn how to create a validator.

PulseChain validators right now are raking in around 10%. It's nice.

62394942
>>62394345
>too expensive
anon, fractional shares exist

62395063
>>62373752
>I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.
Yes, going to college or vocational school and getting a higher paying job is probably the correct path for the vast majority of you.
>t. 32yo engineer making $135k
Go for STEM and if you can't cut it then pick a trade that interests you. If I didn't make it in STEM I was considering being an Air Traffic Controller.

62395577
Brainstorming

Software created current-state AI, AI created current state demand for hardware resources, hardware surge created demand for production materials, hardware costs contribute to higher cost datacentre build costs, running the datacentres with current state AI running on current state hardware consumes higher energy demand.

>AI - priced in
>Hardware - priced in
>Materials - priced in
>Datacentres - priced in
>Energy - priced in

So the disruptor to this ecosystem might be sitting where we started - software development. The race to highly energy-efficient AI models is the next logical thing. The current trajectory is unsustainable from a resources perspective, and the financial burn rate is unsustainable.

You make AI consumption highly resource efficient, all of those things come down as the demand reduces. Or does supply just increase to fill the increased capacity?

That takes me to the next innovative era that will consume the increased capacity;
> Robotics and humanoids / androids - building interest but probably not fully priced in yet
> Transport and automated movement - AI powered, driverless vehicles (short Uber)
> Highly efficient reusable rocketry - transport anything anywhere quickly (reusability powered by AI robotics)
> Personal digital self's - Consumer facing AI-version-of-you running your life, in your house, ordering your groceries - your life on autopilot (think a personal JARVIS)
> medical application - localised AI models in hospitals and medical rooms - control robotics in surgery
> mobile devices - uncertain future, at some point the personal handheld device model may come to an end

So my take away for a macro, strategic tech play could be to accumulate software and tech players with resources that can produce highly efficient AI models that use less resources - Microsoft is trading at a discount? - instead of over-committing to hardware and the fundamentals. Model efficiency could improve at any stage.

Thoughts?

62395589
>>62394870
Don’t you need like 7 figs worth of pulsechain to be a validator

62396733
>>62377801
Why would you want those with over 10K to leave? It sounds like you could use some advice especially if you're five figures and "investing" in trading cards. Your finest minds have more than 10K because they minimally are employed with decent jobs.

62398741
>>62395577
I agree with your thesis that we should accumulate up and coming software players with great ai

that said

> Robotics and humanoids / androids - building interest but probably not fully priced in yet

we're too early

> Transport and automated movement - AI powered, driverless vehicles (short Uber)

https://www.azfamily.com/2026/01/08/waymo-passenger-flees-after-car-drives-phoenix-light-rail-tracks/

I live in AZ, everyone hates Waymo. Uber drivers will literally bully the crap out of them


>Highly efficient reusable rocketry - transport anything anywhere quickly (reusability powered by AI robotics)

Too broad

>Personal digital self's - Consumer facing AI-version-of-you running your life, in your house, ordering your groceries - your life on autopilot (think a personal JARVIS)

This has to be inevitable by the end of the next decade. The writing is on the wall, I mean think of Siri and Alexa, different in scope but has easily prepared consumers to accept the eventuality of "you" just running your own tasks. Small business owners would also love this. Imagine a traveler who stays at an Airbnb month long for business and uploads his AI to manage the boring stuff of his new "home" while he acclimates to the business and travel of the area.


>medical application - localised AI models in hospitals and medical rooms - control robotics in surgery

this has potential

>mobile devices - uncertain future, at some point the personal handheld device model may come to an end

We're too early

62398767
>>62398741
https://www.eurekalert.org/news-releases/1132599

>Publishing in the Journal of the American Medical Association (JAMA) online June 22, the study team found that online portal messages more than doubled between 2020 and 2025 (by 153 percent). By contrast, total telephone calls decreased by 6 percent over the same period.

>“Clinical staff will need to be trained in mastering the tools of messaging in healthcare; in using AI support programs, including chatbots that can frame content to minimize its complexity; and in making the most effective use of clinician time needed for online billing and online counseling,” said Dr. Segev, who is also a profession in NYU Grossman’s Department of Population Health.

>Already, he noted, NYU Langone uses AI support tools to speed up drafting of physician and provider notes.


Doctors already use google to diagnose people

62398789
Some topical reading for today, gentleman, nothing too difficult takes less than 5 mins. Lets take this slow and not bite off more than we can chew

62398798
>>62396733
They'd have no reason to frequent and appreciate these threads or this company. You think rich boomers understand our circumstances?

62398799
Is diversification a meme? Thinking it's better to just go all in on a single stock than an index fund when you have a small account.

62398806
Page 2, come on anon, it doesn't hurt to learn the basics, i'll go over with you, together

62398809
>>62398799
How much are you putting down? I'm thinking of doing $1000 all in picks and making a folder that way

62398813
There, lets read over these three

62398868
>>62398799
You are better off getting a job

62398876
>>62398868
https://voca.ro/1aI6fGYlNRNN

62398905
>>62394942
Don’t get that either. Even if you only bought full shares how could you not afford 200 dollars worth of shares? Is this under 10k net worth or under 100$ net worth

62398985
>>62376699
> you're arguing with a script and losing

62399017
>>62380691
bump

62399272
>>62398789
>Wavelength (λ) (THE SYMBOL IN HALF LIFE)

Distance between repeating points of a wave (e.g., crest to crest). Determines frequency and, for light, color.

>Amplitude:

Maximum height of the wave. Determines intensity (brightness for light, loudness for sound).

>Phase

Position of a wave within its cycle. Determines how waves add or cancel when combined.

>Polarization

Direction of oscillation of a transverse wave. For light, it is the orientation of the electric field.

62399358
Tomorrow we shall give predictive programming a try as photonics will likely be a difficult venture

62399394
10k- networth reporting in! You guys are too smart for me with all those grand ideas.

item.title: KEK tradies are just as fucked by AI as white-collar fags. — 117 replies — 34.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
KEK tradies are just as fucked by AI as white-collar fags.

Open thread • Replies: 117 • Updated: 2026-06-23 04:42 UTC

OP

KEK tradies are just as fucked by AI as white-collar fags. Anthropic found a cheat code to dramatically accelerate robotics using LLMs.

https://www.anthropic.com/research/project-fetch-phase-two

Early replies (first 3)

62392639
So if we're all fucked, what's the endgoal?

62392654
>>62392639
Look what happened to horses

62392668
>>62392639
Cull

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62395049
>>62395039
Oh wow, you really read like you haven’t worked in 40years. Like, in that way you never really had to use your brain or resolve conflict
Y’all are both dummies, lmaoo

62395058
>>62395049
So ignorant and loud lol.You’re a working class in tech lead at amazon. lmao.

62395098
>>62395058
I just calls ‘em like i sees ‘em. Two dummies in slapfight

62395120
still can't even get over 1.5% on arc agi 3, ai still can't generalize, you'll get specialized use cases but that's it, it's still extremely expensive to train it to do anything useful

62395135
>>62393981
I always act nice to the tradies, give them seriously good food etc. They always leave ton of trash, cig butts, break shit etc. I wonder how bad things would be without the good coffee, snacks and kindness. I too welcome the robots.

62395154
>>62395010
There is no way that poster is real, he has to be AI agent talking to all the other ones on here (most of the "posters").

IS ANYONE REAL HERE? HENLO!?

62395201
>>62393713
Thts dumb as fuck. We already have premade buildings...you make them with cranes. No faggot robots needed. You make the crane automated...at your own peril.

62396828
>>62395154
Someone has to be real

62397022
>>62395010
yes, this is yet another balding discord tranny OCPD namenigger troll thread.

62397302
>>62392639
You will be left to eat cockroaches out of trash cans while the elites take your underage daughter back to their utopia to fuck and kill for pleasure. Oh, and you won't do shit about it either. Enjoy!

62397384
>>62395201
The world is sick of low iq blue collar workers.

62397402
>>62392639
the singularity
massive central processing warehouses where people will go to merge their consciousness with the computer

62397409
>>62395015
>I have access to self-hosted AGI too
May we see it? Your Claude mythos and blackwell systems?

62397444
>>62397302
They do. The masses are roleplaying being good but their nature is way darker than the elite's. That's the real
>infrared pill

62397478
>>62397444
The masses are low iq and poor.

62397486
>>62392639
pimp

62398293
>>62397478
Which is worse than being evil.

62398312
>>62392533
All the traders I talk to have fucked up knees and backs so not only are they just as worse off but they have fucked up bodies as well

62398353
>>62398293
My daddy always told me being poor is a crime

62398878
>>62392639
Dutch Disease but literally every country, especially the ones who invested the most in education.
Like how a petrostate does the bare minimum of investing in their own population and focuses 99% of their money on improving oil extraction.
AI countries will do the bare minimum of investing in their human populations, with 99% of their money going towards improving the AIs.
You can already kind of see this with the US and China. China gives its citizens jack shit but will happily invest in more robots. The US is sort of the same but more emphasis on thinking machines than robots.
Any country that doesn’t is going to be uncompetitive, though over time the AI dutch disease countries will also become uncompetitive in their own ways as the corruption and poverty rots them within.
In order to prevent mass poverty we’ll need to (in the short term) try to follow the norway model more than the typical petrostate model.
Like how Norway used its oil money to juice its sovereign wealth fund, we’ll need to use AI money on a sovereign wealth fund in order to keep our citizens from completely languishing away.
Long term we need to find ways to synergize human existence with machine existence. So that individuals can also benefit from the endless investment that will be put into the machines.

62398883
Oh look, another lie to prop up the stock price.

62398888
>>62392639
90% into the gulag they are literally unusable
Remaining 9% slave race because they happen to specialize in some field on par or greater to an LLM. They will serve the the remaining 1%

62398909
>>62392759
>>62394987
Why do anons always assume rich fags keep people alive? That they’ll just snap their fingers and everyone dies?
If the rich go for a segregated AI economy, then non AI using people will just continue to live on.
The “poors“ will even build AI robots of their own given enough time.

62398916
>>62393510
sure but how many humans are we talking
certainly not 8 gorillion

62398939
>>62398909
This is the first time in recorded history that a tool threatens with replacing humans as a mean of production. I know some of you dont understand the implications of this, but to pretend this scenario has happened before is retarded.
An AGI powered robot will be able to replace you, me and everyone else. You will not be able to develop your own AGI, because why would the elite give you means for independence? They are sociopaths, they only want you for your money, they are right now threatening you with your replacement telling you they want to replace you straight to your face.

62399166
>>62398939
>You will not be able to develop your own AGI
Kek, wanna bet on that?

62399211
blue collar is 4 losers

62399258
>>62393534
not him but the asian twink in the t-shirt, hes cute. probably has a 2m nw. would like to have sex with him.

62399375
>>62399166
Yes, i bet you don't even have the brain to finetune an open weights model, let alone develop something that still doesn't exist and will (probably) require a new computer hardware paradigm to exist.

62399432
Why can't finance fags understand economics ? It's not that robots can't do wagie shit, they absolutely can they just can't do it economically.

item.title: Irl crime — 42 replies — 22.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Irl crime

Open thread • Replies: 42 • Updated: 2026-06-23 06:10 UTC

OP

Share crime stories and currently relevant opportunities ITT. IRL shit, not online scamming.

Inb4
>muh bankers muh politicians
Wow, very funny, what an original joke

Early replies (first 3)

62393319
>>62393307
I fucked your mom.

62393322
12 years ago my first job was in a supermarket. I randomly decided to steal a candy bar for no reason, but i felt so guilty that I confessed to my supervisor a day later and offered to pay. She said it was okay, and nothing happened.

62393326
>>62393319
This guy paid me $50 so he could fuck your mom.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62393519
Bump

62393717
Bump

62393826
>>62393307
I'm sure beds for pigs would be fine if the pigs were providing some kind of valued service

62394022
I stole over 20k worth of gold from my former best friends father's safe because he wrote the fucking code on paper and it was in his nightstand. Drove to neighboring country to sell it for cash. By the time I heard about it enough time had passed that I don't think they ever suspected me. My friend was telling me his dad was pissed and accused their house cleaner lol.

62394033
>>62393307
Find gov depots and ebay their shit. You paid for it.

62395766
I took 64GB of RAM from an unused computer at work and put it in my home computer. I needed it

62395812
>>62393307
wipepo always going around starting bogus wars based on lies, looking for wmd where there aren't any, but meanwhile israel has the wmd, but white trash are quiet about it. they're too scared to even mention it. all problems come from white trash males

62395828
in college (over 20 years ago) I grew mushrooms in a big rubbermaid tote. kept getting insane flushes and had about 6 pounds dry. sold it to an underclassman very cheap, like 120 an ounce. I assume he was just reselling it. blew most of the money helping a friend who was in film school to produce his senior thesis short.

62396109
>>62393424
Nice one. I bet that happens all the time.

When I was in high school in Minecraft everyone wanted shrooms but there was no source for them so I started ordering Aminita muscaria online and sold it to a dealer in bulk. He bought bags of it from me every week for about five weeks and then finally told me my shit sucks and people wanted their money back. I still made thousands and in high school that’s a lot.

62396291
>>62393307
I've unironically heavily considered card fraud considering how easy it is to do and the fact that literal gangbanging hood negroes do it regularly and get at worst a mild punishment for a fraction of the money stolen
I just don't want to get raped and glock dookied in prison so I'm too scared to commit

62396405
>>62395828
How the fuck did you not have mold contaminate the crates??I struggled endlessly with this back in the day

62396415
>>62396291
Pussy

I knew quite a few lads that went into that and made a buck

62396427
>>62396415
>lads
the US is soft on cc fraud but the UK is even more so desu
I know your fraud yutes over there rarely even get time but here they'll get a year or two at least so the government can pretend they're doing something

62396438
>>62393307
I eat poop

62396596
>>62396427
Jail is ok

62396641
>>62396405
it is difficult to keep contamination out but I would do things to mitigate like using a lot of isopropyl alcohol to clean surfaces, using a glove box for inoculation, etc. I do think when you spawn seed to pasteurized manure is the part that a real roll of the dice. just so many opportunities to introduce contaminants there.

62396900
>>62393356
>I meant torturing someone for crypto, armed robbery etc
There are two reasons you'd be this up front about such topics here
>honeypot trap
>low civilization tier subhuman

62396921
This thread glows

62397020
>>62396900
I'm a low civilization tier subhuman. I don't give a fuck about your gay "civilization.

62397200
>>62393307
When I was a young teenager, I had a friend who bought a bb gun pistol. He then waited for evening, went to the park, hid behind the bushes near a path, and then jumped out at old people walking and stole their wallets and jewelry. He made a lot of money. He asked me to join in, but I wasn't interested.

No idea what happened to him. Probably dead. Good riddance.

62397518
Bump

62397557
>>62394022
What a literal fucking nigger. Repent

62397565
>>62396900
Retards posting obviously fake quotes is always funny

62397959
>>62397557
Kys

62398384
>>62393307
Me on the picrel

62398550
>>62393307
>>62393356
>>62394022
>>62395812
>>62397020
>>62397565
>>62397959
Izzat farmers
Abandon thread

62398943
Bump

62399147
>>62393307
Sleep Tight Pigger

62399469
This one time my bros and i knew about 35 mil worth of gold bars so we painted the floor beneath their safe with explosives and then had a decoy boat drive off as we cracked the safe in the water below. Then we scuba'd the bars to a seperate boat, and wbile celebrating one of our own bros decided to double cross us as we celebrated our heist. Fuckin loser didnt even know what to do with all that money besides copy all our ideas. So anyway we come up with a plan to steal the gold back by sending our dead safe cracker's daughter (whom the coward was unfamiliar with) to sneak into his house disguised as a television internet repair woman, and we cut the lines of his tv. Our plans were almost ruined when his neighbors had a house party so we called it off and had to rethink our plans; we surveilled him and found out he was moving our gold in 3 trucks so we modded 3 mini coopers all souped up and pulled another demolished floor trick, the loser viewing from the helicopter couldnt see because we had a billboard truck that was blocking the view of the gold truck falling into the subway below. We knew which truck the gold was in because the ride height and our hackerbro friend basically led them right into a traffic jam above the explosive floor. After incapacitating the drivers we cracked the safe and transported the gold in our minis to a train. Our former betrayor tried to buy his way onto the train only to find that we paid off the mobsters that owned the train a ton of cash and gold to be part of our plan too. Then we listened to money by pink floyd.

62399612
>>62399469

That hackerbro's name? Albert Einstein.

item.title: Crypto-Hopium — 22 replies — 13.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Crypto-Hopium

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-06-23 06:27 UTC

OP

>The CLARITY Act is about to be signed.
US corporations will begin buying large quantities of Bitcoin and altcoins.
>The US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve will accumulate 5% of the total supply using a rebalanced Strategic Gold Reserve as collateral.
Trump is about to audit Fort Knox, and ARMA is being drafted to prevent future presidents from selling for a minimum of 20 years.
>The housing market has introduced cryptocurrency backed mortgages.
Participants will hold their cryptocurrencies for 15 and 30 year terms.

Early replies (first 3)

62393159
>The CLARITY Act is about to be signed

really nigga? I thought this shit was dead for this year

62393165
>>62393089
Clarity Act isn't going through. It's completely stalled in negotiations and they've got a week to get something like 10 dems to cross lines.

62393203
>>62393165
According to whom?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62393204
>>62393089
Funny how you niggers always desperately need a meme date/event/regulation/narrative, this board is 99% indians who cannot think for themselves.

62393377
>>62393204
It's funnier how you can read the OP and disregard it as if it has zero positive implications for the cryptocurrency market.

62394871
>>62393089
The government won’t buy anything until everyone capitulated nigger. I’m almost certain it will dump and a final capitulation will begin.

62394904
>>62393203
the recess begins on june 29 and runs to july 10. after that they once again have only 20 days to pass it before the august 1 recess that goes into september, which is midterm season.
>10 dems
the bill does have opponents and it isn't filibuster-proof without some dems voting it in. plus it needs to be bipartisan or it's going to be doa regardless. every dem that has signaled support has done so contingent on some kind of concession, few of which have been addressed

62394929
>>62393089
Trump was meant to be the crypto president and the first thing he did when he took office was rugpull his followers with a shitcoin, ruining whatever goodwill crypto might have had at that point. It's not happening. It's over.

62395050
>>62393204

we need the banks to buy the coins that kill the banks

it's easy, see?

62396159
>>62393089
Cryptobros just don't understand that 10/10 was curtain call for crypto

62397222
>>62393089
What kind of drooling retard actually thinks clarity will make crypto pump?

You are convincing NOBODY

62397241
>>62396159
cryptobros have been exterminated. I think the only people still remotely bullish are orthodox cycle believers.

62397263
>>62397241
>>62396159
crypto will be dead for at least another year. Maybe bitcoin can get back over 100k in 2028, but its dead in the water for many months to come. Imagine holding crypto since last year when you could have been getting rich in the stock market. Sorry crypto bros. Its over. And YOU LOST.

62398703
>>62397222
drooling retard here...
I do

62398770
>>62397222
>What kind of drooling retard actually thinks clarity will make crypto pump?

Anon, it gives banks the greenlight and all the compliance laws they need to start messing around with a bunch of crypto tech they've been developing for years.

62399414
>>62393089
I don't understand, the clarity act doesn't help bitcoin in any way. Only shitcoiners care about this getting passed.

62399467
>>62393203
Well I've found Eleanor Terret useful on the act, she's the fox news crypto correspondent or some shit and has good white house insight. She called it passing out of the house accurately but is saying it's currently just dead and won't make the july date.

62399527
>>62398770
>Banking Cryptotech
>Look inside
>It's just SPAXX but on a blockchain
You retards really are getting hyped for fucking money market funds. This is almost as embarrassing as the euros that brag about their high yield savings account yield.

62399531
>>62399527
>mfw they invented a worse way to do what your bank already does

kek, enjoy your 4% with extra steps anon

62399584
>>62393089
I hope so /biz has been a massive bottom signal lately.

62399617
I really feel like it won't pass. No one cares about crypto right now.

62399643
>>62399617
> crypto is dead until it isn't. you're just early to the next cycle.
